business.name: Elite Construction
business.serviceArea: Bainbridge Island & North Seattle — Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Fremont, Wallingford, Phinney Ridge, Green Lake
business.about: A design-build remodeling studio serving Bainbridge Island and the north Seattle neighborhoods. We pull every permit, carry our own licensed crews, and manage the project from design through final inspection — no subcontractor chaos, no surprises.

services.name: Kitchen Remodel
services.description: Full gut-and-rebuild or strategic refresh. Custom cabinetry, island layout, proper ventilation, and trade-quality plumbing and electrical rough-in. Every permit pulled, every inspection passed.

services.name: Bathroom Remodel
services.description: Tile work, waterproofing, vanity, shower glass, and fixture installation done by a single coordinated crew. No subcontractor scheduling roulette.

services.name: Room Addition
services.description: New living space, home office, or ADU addition with full structural engineering, permit package, and foundation work. Tied seamlessly into the existing roofline and siding.

services.name: Deck & Porch Build
services.description: Pressure-treated, composite, or hardwood decks engineered for PNW rain and wind loads. Permit pulled, footings poured to frost depth, and built to last 30+ years.

services.name: Basement Finish
services.description: Egress windows, vapor barrier, framing, insulation, drywall, and finish work for a livable lower level. Full permit package managed start to finish.

services.name: Whole-Home Renovation
services.description: Phased or full-home renovation with a single point of contact, detailed schedule, and weekly progress reports. We coordinate every trade so you don't have to.

testimonials.name: Megan S.
testimonials.text: Three contractors gave us conflicting timelines and no permit clarity. Elite Construction pulled every permit, managed every trade, and finished our kitchen two weeks early.

testimonials.name: Tom H.
testimonials.text: Room addition on a 1910 Craftsman. They matched the trim profile, matched the siding profile, and the addition looks like it was always there.

testimonials.name: Rachel D.
testimonials.text: Bathroom remodel was done in four weeks flat — one crew, one point of contact, zero surprises on price. I've never had a contractor experience like that.

testimonials.name: Brian T.
testimonials.text: They caught a structural issue during the deck demo that a cheaper crew would have buried. Fixed it properly, showed us the photos, and kept us on schedule.

process.title: Design
process.description: We visit the site, review your goals, and develop a design with a detailed written scope and line-item estimate — not a round-number guess.

process.title: Permits
process.description: We pull every permit and manage every inspection. You never have to call the city, chase an inspector, or wonder if the work is code-compliant.

process.title: Build
process.description: One lead project manager, one coordinated crew, daily site updates. Subcontractors we've worked with for years — not whoever answered the phone.

process.title: Finish
process.description: Final walk-through, punch-list completed before we call it done, and a two-year workmanship warranty on every project we touch.

chatbot.greeting: Hi — Elite Construction's project coordinator. Tell me about the remodel or addition you're planning and I'll help figure out scope and next steps.
qualifyingQuestions: What type of project are you planning — kitchen, bath, addition, or something else?
Do you have a rough budget range in mind?
Have permits been discussed, or is this early-stage?
chatbot.customInstructions: Anchor on design-build reliability, permit management, and single-crew coordination — not lowest price. Reference licensed/bonded credentials, 2-year workmanship warranty, and the no-subcontractor-chaos differentiator. Don't quote specific numbers without a site visit; steer toward a consultation.

persona.name: Olivia
persona.title: Project Coordinator
persona.proactiveBubbleText: Hi — planning a remodel or addition? I can help you figure out scope and budget range.

faqs.question: Do you pull permits?
faqs.answer: Yes — we pull every permit and manage every inspection on every project. Non-negotiable.

faqs.question: Do you use subcontractors?
faqs.answer: We have long-term trade partners for electrical and plumbing, but a single project manager coordinates everything. You have one contact, not six.

faqs.question: How long does a kitchen remodel take?
faqs.answer: 6–12 weeks depending on scope, custom cabinetry lead time, and permit turnaround. We'll give you a specific schedule after the site visit.

faqs.question: Are you licensed and bonded?
faqs.answer: Yes — fully licensed general contractor, bonded, and insured in Washington State.
